title: ptelea trifoliata f. aurea
intro: Ptelea trifoliata f. aurea, also known as the Golden Prickly Ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree in the family Rutaceae, characterized by its golden foliage and fragrant flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Ptelea trifoliata f. aurea include its ability to relieve pain and reduce inflammation, as well as its potential to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, can be made from its leaves, roots, and bark, although more research is needed to fully understand its uses and potential interactions.
